newsSinceJun6: No material company news since June 6. Latest disclosure was the June 3 voting-rights/share-count notice (15.4M shares as of May 29). Today's -7.4% move appears flow-driven profit-taking after a ~62% one-month / +812% YTD run in a $0.21B microcap, not fundamental. Standing overhangs unchanged: OpenChip may request issuance of new shares by July 31, 2026 to take a 51% majority stake (dilution/ownership-reset overhang); cash was EUR 2.9M at end-2025 with funding horizon stated only 'beyond June 30, 2026'; AGM scheduled June 29, 2026.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Kalray ALKAL.PA news & quote
AInvest - Kalray's OpenChip warrant overhang could reset ownership and pressure shares
Finanzwire - Kalray voting rights and shares as of May 29, 2026
Actusnews - Kalray FY2025 annual results (EBITDA EUR 6.2M, new scope)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already applied the post-rerating crowding penalty (down); today's -7.4% is a flow-driven giveback with no new fundamentals and only trims a sliver of an +812% YTD run, so it does not materially restore asymmetry. The July 31 OpenChip 51% issuance option and thin cash runway cap the DPU-optionality upside, keeping this a hold with mild negative drift rather than a fresh downgrade.
verdicts.bull: Kalray remains one of the few listed pure-play DPU/data-processing assets in Europe with FY2025 swinging to positive EBITDA (EUR 6.2M) and a EUR 10M OpenChip cooperation agreement validating the technology; any new design win or AI-gigafactory contract could re-ignite the move.
verdicts.neutral: No news since June 6; the -7.4% day is profit-taking in a retail-momentum microcap after a 62% month, and the setup is unchanged until the June 29 AGM and the July 31 OpenChip warrant decision resolve the ownership question.
verdicts.bear: An +812% YTD rerating on a EUR 2.9M end-2025 cash position with a funding horizon barely past June 30, 2026 leaves the equity hostage to OpenChip's 51% issuance option - heavy dilution or a controlled takeover at terms unfavorable to minorities could erase most residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: OpenChip's decision by July 31, 2026 on exercising its right to subscribe new shares for a 51% majority stake (plus the June 29 AGM resolutions framing it).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dilution/ownership reset from the OpenChip 51% issuance combined with a thin liquidity runway, against a position already rerated +812% YTD.
